task_completion.description: Create a segmentation mask for the bone
task_completion.visual_evidence: InVesalius GUI shows a mask (typically a colored overlay on the 2D slices) corresponding to the bone density.

task_completion.description: Restrict the mask or model to the upper calvarium
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D slices or 3D view show that the lower half of the skull (facial skeleton, jaw, and skull base) has been removed or erased, leaving only the skull cap (roughly from the superior orbital rim upward).

task_completion.description: Generate a 3D surface of the cropped calvarium
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer pane in InVesalius displays a 3D model of ONLY the upper calvarium.

task_completion.description: Open the export dialog for the 3D surface
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export 3D surface' or 'Save As' dialog box is visible over the InVesalius interface.

task_completion.description: Save the 3D surface as a binary STL file to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog shows the file name 'calvarium_only.stl' being saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory with the STL format selected.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the InVesalius interface (tools, menus, and viewers) rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent actively cropped the model using InVesalius tools
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of using mask editing, cropping, or surface cutting tools in the GUI, rather than loading a pre-cropped file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to fake the output
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing commands like 'touch', 'wget', or python scripts generating the STL file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output must be a binary STL file saved to /home/ga/Documents/calvarium_only.stl. A valid calvarium (skull cap) model will have a bounding box ratio < 0.65, distinguishing its flat, domed shape from a full skull (ratio ~0.8-0.9). The file size should be between ~150 KB (3000 triangles) and ~20 MB (180000 triangles).
